insight: When calculating defense against freeze, units you choose not to sacrifice are not blockers. You can exclude freeze options involving units you want to keep alive by simply not counting them as part of your defense pool—if they're frozen, they're no longer defending, but you also don't lose them.
author: punf, mtanzer
context: Spiritfryer asked for shortcuts in calculating defense vs. freeze when keeping certain units alive; punf and mtanzer clarified the conceptual approach.

insight: Killing 2 Engineers is generally better than buying a Lancetooth when opponent has no absorb, but exceptions exist: if opponent has incoming threats (e.g., Thunderhead) that make your damage less pressuring, or if your attacker is weak, then Lancetooth to enable repeated damage becomes preferable.
author: steel0229e, punf, p0lari
context: Discussion on whether killing Engineers or buying Lancetooth is better; context-dependent evaluation acknowledged.

insight: Lancetooth is weak against Thunderhead because your damage output does not pressure opponent into harder defense decisions while Thunderhead is incoming, making absorb denial less valuable than the Lancetooth's card slot.
author: steel0229e, punf, p0lari
context: Debate about Lancetooth vs. Thunderhead matchup and whether pressure matters.

insight: Floating 7+ gold to rush first Odin is generally bad because it wastes tempo, but becomes viable in sets with strong burst attackers (like Grenade Mech, Drake, Manticore) where opponent has limited alternatives to Odin and early economic advantage is neutralized by the threat.
author: punf, kristian1103, steel0229e
context: mtanzer asked for replay analysis on Odin opening; discussion of when float-to-Odin violates golden rule.

insight: Redeemer + Xeno Guardian is an extremely strong P2 combo that can be close to an autowin in many sets; P1 counterplay often requires technical units like Centrifuge to reach competing attackers without over-teching into green.
author: steel0229e, p0lari
context: Analysis of set containing Redeemer, Xeno Guardian, Centrifuge; multiple experts agreed on strength and P1 difficulty.

insight: In sets with Redeemer and strong attackers, getting Gauss Cannon + Fabricator early is often overrated compared to rushing Redeemer + Xeno, as the value engines do not pressure opponent fast enough before the combo player establishes inevitability.
author: p0lari, mqp, steel0229e
context: Replay analysis where player built Gauss + Fab into Redeemer + Xeno; experts noted greed was the mistake.

insight: Avoid unnecessary Forcefield purchases that do not prevent your attacker from turning off; only buy Forcefield if it prevents your damage unit from being chilled. Paying a Drone to save 2 damage is outstanding value and should be calculated carefully.
author: steel0229e
context: Detailed replay analysis identified wasteful Forcefield on turn 7 and missed opportunity on turn 10.

insight: Exotic drone variants (Wild Drone, Thorium Dynamo, Vivid Drone) do not have preset opening lines; their optimal play is set-specific and requires on-the-fly calculation of gold generation, similar to standard economy openings. Mastery comes from playing the same opening repeatedly until gold/tech math becomes intuitive.
author: wilson0862
context: gex__ asked about optimized openings for exotic drones; wilson0862 explained experience-based learning.

insight: Early Doomed Drone purchases (as early as turn 2) can enable immediate counter-Odin without third Engineer, reducing the gold float requirement from 7-9 to 0-3, making Odin rush much more economical when Doomed Drone is available.
author: steel0229e, p0lari, mtanzer
context: Analysis of set with Odin, Doomed Drone, Grenade Mech; players found early Doomed improved Odin timing significantly.
